title: Toast and ReloadPrompt improvements: auto-dismiss, queue, dirty-check, ARIA
analysis:
This task covers 9 improvements across Toast and ReloadPrompt components.

**Toast (5 issues):**
- TO-1: No auto-dismiss timeout. Toast stays forever. Fix: add setTimeout auto-dismiss (3s success/info, 5s error) inside the Toast component using onMount/onCleanup.
- TO-2: Only one toast at a time (single string signal). Fix: Convert App.tsx toastMessage/toastType to a ToastMessage[] array signal. ToastMessage type already exists in types.ts with {id, type, message}. Toast component renders from queue, individual dismiss removes by id.
- TO-3: No aria-live region. Fix: Add role='alert' and aria-live='assertive' to the toast root div.
- TO-4: Close button lacks accessible label. Fix: Add aria-label='Close' to the x button.
- TO-5: No enter/exit animation. Fix: Add CSS keyframe animation for slide-in from right.

**ReloadPrompt (3 issues):**
- R-1: Only one file tracked (single string signal). Fix: Convert changedFile to a Set<string> signal. ReloadPrompt props change from filePath: string|null to filePaths: string[]. Show count for multiple files.
- R-2: No keyboard shortcut for accept/dismiss. Fix: Add Ctrl+Shift+R for reload and Escape for dismiss via useKeyboardShortcuts hook.
- R-4: Reload destroys unsaved editor changes without warning. Fix: Check editorStore.state.dirtyFiles before reload. If dirty files overlap with changed files, show a confirmation warning.

**Review fix (blocking):**
- partial_failure_state_corruption: handleReload uses Promise.all which rejects on any single failure, leaving changedFiles in an inconsistent state (successfully-reloaded files remain in the set, so the reload prompt stays visible for already-reloaded files). Fix: Use Promise.allSettled, clear only succeeded files from changedFiles, keep failed files in the set, show error toast for failures, and always reset confirmReload.

**Key observations:**
- ToastMessage type already exists in types.ts with {id, type, message} — use directly for queue.
- editorStore already has dirtyFiles: string[] — check intersection with changedFiles for R-4.
- useKeyboardShortcuts hook has a clean pattern for adding shortcuts — extend for R-2.
- Existing tests use @solidjs/testing-library with vitest, JSDOM environment.
- App.test.tsx has comprehensive mocking of bridge/viewport/editor — will extend existing patterns.
- showToast(message, type) helper already exists in App.tsx scope — use for error toasts on partial failure.

prerequisites.description: Verify vitest and @solidjs/testing-library work in the worktree by running existing Toast and ReloadPrompt tests

steps.description: Write failing test for TO-4: Toast close button should have aria-label='Close'. Assert that the button inside the toast has the correct aria-label attribute.
Add aria-label='Close' to the x button in Toast.tsx
Write failing test for TO-3: Toast root div should have role='alert' and aria-live='assertive'. Assert both attributes exist on the toast element.
Add role='alert' and aria-live='assertive' to the toast root div in Toast.tsx

Write failing tests for TO-1: Toast auto-dismiss. Use vi.useFakeTimers. Tests: (1) success toast calls onDismiss after 3000ms, (2) error toast calls onDismiss after 5000ms, (3) info toast calls onDismiss after 3000ms, (4) manual dismiss before timeout does not double-call onDismiss (clearTimeout on cleanup).

Add auto-dismiss setTimeout in Toast.tsx using onMount/onCleanup. Compute timeout from props.type (3000ms for success/info, 5000ms for error). Call props.onDismiss() on timeout. Clear timeout in onCleanup.

Write failing test for TO-5: Toast should have a CSS animation applied. Verify the toast element's computed/applied class includes the animation styling (check for animation-name or that the CSS module class is applied correctly).
Add @keyframes slideIn animation to Toast.module.css (translateX(100%) + opacity 0 -> translateX(0) + opacity 1 over 200ms ease-out). Apply animation property to .toast class.

Write failing tests for TO-2: Toast queue support in App. Tests: (1) showToast adds a ToastMessage to the toasts array, (2) two sequential showToast calls result in two toasts rendered, (3) dismissing one toast by id removes only that toast, (4) handleDoExport success creates a toast in the queue. This requires refactoring App.tsx toast state from single signal to array.

In App.tsx: Replace toastMessage/toastType signals with a toasts signal of ToastMessage[]. Add showToast(message, type) helper that generates unique id (counter-based) and appends. Update handleDismissToast to accept id and filter. Update handleDoExport to use showToast(). Render with <For> over toasts array, each rendering a <Toast>. Update Toast props to accept ToastMessage (id + message + type). Update App.module.css toastContainer to stack with flex-direction: column-reverse and gap: 8px.

Write failing tests for R-1: ReloadPrompt multi-file tracking. Tests: (1) renders nothing when filePaths is empty array, (2) renders single file basename when filePaths has one entry, (3) renders 'N files changed on disk' when filePaths has 2+ entries, (4) Reload and Dismiss buttons still work.

Update ReloadPrompt props from filePath: string|null to filePaths: string[]. Show when array is non-empty. Display basename for single file, 'N files changed on disk. Reload?' for multiple. Update existing tests to use new prop name.

Write failing tests for R-1 App integration: In App.tsx, changedFiles should be a Set<string> signal. Tests: (1) onFileChanged with two different files adds both to the set, (2) onFileChanged with same file twice only has one entry, (3) handleReload reloads all files in set, (4) handleDismissReload clears the set. Update existing App.test.tsx tests that reference changedFile/filePath.

In App.tsx: Convert changedFile signal to changedFiles as createSignal<Set<string>>(new Set()). Update onFileChanged handler to add to set via new Set([...prev, path]). Pass Array.from(changedFiles()) as filePaths to ReloadPrompt. Update handleReload to iterate all files in set, calling bridgeOpenFile for each. Update handleDismissReload to reset to empty set. Fix any broken existing App tests.

Write failing tests for R-4: dirty-file check before reload. Tests: (1) when no dirty files overlap, handleReload proceeds immediately (bridgeOpenFile called), (2) when dirty files overlap with changed files, handleReload shows a confirmation warning (does NOT call bridgeOpenFile immediately), (3) confirming 'Reload Anyway' triggers bridgeOpenFile for all changed files, (4) dismissing confirmation does not reload.

In App.tsx handleReload: Check intersection of changedFiles with editorStore.state.dirtyFiles. If overlap exists, set a confirmReload signal to true. In ReloadPrompt: accept optional hasDirtyFiles prop. When true, show warning text 'Unsaved changes will be lost' and change Reload button text to 'Reload Anyway'. When false or absent, show normal UI. In App.tsx, pass hasDirtyFiles computed from intersection. handleReload only proceeds to bridgeOpenFile when confirmReload is set or no dirty overlap. Update ReloadPrompt.module.css with warning styling.

Write failing tests for R-2: keyboard shortcuts. Tests: (1) Ctrl+Shift+R dispatched on document calls onReloadShortcut callback, (2) Escape dispatched on document calls onDismissReload callback, (3) shortcuts are skipped when target is an input element.

Extend KeyboardShortcutCallbacks in useKeyboardShortcuts.ts with onReloadShortcut and onDismissReload. Add handlers: Ctrl+Shift+R -> onReloadShortcut, Escape -> onDismissReload. In App.tsx, wire onReloadShortcut to handleReload (only when changedFiles non-empty) and onDismissReload to handleDismissReload (only when reload prompt visible).

Write failing tests for partial_failure_state_corruption fix in handleReload. Add new describe block 'App handleReload partial failure' in App.test.tsx with tests: (1) when one bridgeOpenFile rejects and another succeeds, only the failed file remains in changedFiles (the succeeded file is removed from the set), (2) when one file fails, confirmReload is still reset to false, (3) when one file fails, an error toast is shown via showToast mentioning the failed file count, (4) when all files succeed, changedFiles is cleared completely (existing behavior preserved), (5) when all files fail, all files remain in changedFiles. Mock bridge.openFile to resolve for some paths and reject for others using mockImplementation with conditional logic.

In App.tsx handleReload: Replace Promise.all with Promise.allSettled. After settlement, partition results into fulfilled and rejected. For fulfilled results, their corresponding files are removed from changedFiles. For rejected results, their files remain in changedFiles. Always call setConfirmReload(false) regardless of outcome. If any promises rejected, call showToast with an error message indicating N file(s) failed to reload. If all succeeded, setChangedFiles(new Set()). If partial failure, setChangedFiles(new Set(failedPaths)). The editorStore.updateFileContent calls remain in individual .then() handlers (before allSettled) so successfully-loaded files still get their content updated immediately.

design_decisions.decision: Use existing ToastMessage type from types.ts for the toast queue
design_decisions.rationale: The type already has {id, type, message} which is exactly what we need for a queue. Reusing it avoids type duplication.

design_decisions.decision: Auto-dismiss timeouts: 3s for success/info, 5s for error
design_decisions.rationale: Success messages are informational and can disappear quickly. Error messages need more reading time. These are standard UX timeouts.

design_decisions.decision: Toast auto-dismiss via onMount/onCleanup inside Toast component rather than in App
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the timer lifecycle tied to the component lifecycle. Each Toast manages its own timeout, which is cleaner for a queue where toasts have independent lifetimes.

design_decisions.decision: Convert changedFile to a Set<string> signal rather than an array
design_decisions.rationale: Prevents duplicates when the same file changes multiple times before the user acts. Set semantics are correct for 'which files have changed'.

design_decisions.decision: Dirty-file check shows a warning state in ReloadPrompt via hasDirtyFiles prop rather than a separate modal
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps the UX lightweight and non-modal. The ReloadPrompt already occupies the right screen area for this warning. Adding a 'Reload Anyway' variant is less disruptive than a modal dialog.

design_decisions.decision: Add keyboard shortcuts via extending useKeyboardShortcuts rather than local listeners
design_decisions.rationale: Centralizes all keyboard shortcuts in one place, consistent with existing pattern. Avoids scattered event listeners.

design_decisions.decision: Toast animation via CSS @keyframes rather than JS animation library
design_decisions.rationale: CSS animations are performant and don't add bundle size. The animation is simple (slide-in from right) and doesn't need JS orchestration.

design_decisions.decision: Stack toasts with flex-direction column-reverse in the container
design_decisions.rationale: Newest toasts appear at the bottom (closest to the container's anchor point at bottom-right). This is the standard toast stack UX pattern.

design_decisions.decision: Toast id generation uses a simple incrementing counter rather than crypto.randomUUID
design_decisions.rationale: Counter is deterministic and testable. UUIDs are overkill for in-memory component state that doesn't persist.

design_decisions.decision: Use Promise.allSettled instead of Promise.all for handleReload to handle partial failures gracefully
design_decisions.rationale: Promise.all short-circuits on first rejection, leaving successfully-reloaded files stuck in changedFiles. Promise.allSettled waits for all promises and lets us partition succeeded vs failed files. Only failed files remain in changedFiles, keeping UI state consistent with actual editor state. confirmReload is always reset regardless of outcome.

design_decisions.decision: Track file-to-promise mapping by index correlation with Array.from(files) for allSettled result processing
design_decisions.rationale: Promise.allSettled returns results in the same order as the input array. By iterating Array.from(files) alongside allSettled results, we can determine exactly which paths succeeded and which failed without needing a separate tracking structure.

reuse.how: Already defined with {id, type, message}. Use directly for the toast queue array signal in App.tsx. Import in Toast.tsx for props.
Already tracks dirty files as string[]. Check intersection with changedFiles for R-4 dirty-file check before reload.
Extend KeyboardShortcutCallbacks interface with onReloadShortcut and onDismissReload. Add Ctrl+Shift+R and Escape handlers following the existing Ctrl+O, F5, Ctrl+E pattern.
Reuse the same vi.mock setup for bridge, viewport, editor mocks when testing new App integration features (toast queue, multi-file reload, dirty check).
Follow same render/screen/fireEvent patterns for new tests. Use vi.useFakeTimers for timeout tests.
Continue using --reify-* CSS custom properties for theming consistency in any new styles.
Already exists in handleReload's scope. Use showToast(message, 'error') to surface partial reload failures as error toasts to the user, leveraging the toast queue infrastructure built in steps 9-10.
